w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh wp14

w:r.w:t: Here is the revised implementation plan reflecting the shift to a custom React.js ingestion page and a pure Python backend, completely removing the n8n dependency.

This document serves as the tactical execution plan for developing and deploying the AI Journalist platform. It translates the High-Level and Low-Level architecture designs into actionable development sprints, ensuring a systematic transition from manual prompt validation to a fully standalone ingestion and retrieval pipeline powered exclusively by React.js and Python.
The rollout is divided into three consecutive phases to isolate risks and validate the core thought-leadership conversational loop before scaling the backend infrastructure.
Phase 1: Manual RAG & Persona Validation (Current - Day 2/Day 3)

Focus on prompt engineering and manual context injection. Validate that the LLM correctly assumes the AI Journalist persona and generates high-quality follow-up questions when provided with an expert answer and manual data snippets.

Establish the backend infrastructure. Build the custom React.js ingestion page for YouTube links and develop the pure Python extraction and retrieval functions to replace the manual context injection.
Finalize the client-side chat interface, integrate real-time Speech-to-Text (STT) capabilities, and connect the end-to-end event loop through the
The following matrix outlines task distribution to ensure parallel development without
Day 2/Day 3 Manual RAG execution, transcript sharing, and prompt testing.
Pure Python YouTube transcript extraction, embedding generation, and raw SQL ingestion pipelines.
React.js frontend development, including the new YouTube Ingestion Page, 10-rule UI/UX chat framework, and architecture review.
Note: Ensure a review session is scheduled with Sir to validate the prompt chaining results at the conclusion of Phase 1.
(PostgreSQL) instance. Set up the schemas required to store parent-child data structures for Hierarchical RAG (HRAG).
Build a dedicated React.js portal where team members can manually paste YouTube URLs (podcasts, expert interviews). This page will send the URLs directly to the Python backend.
Write pure Python scripts (using libraries like
) to receive the links, download the VTT/SRT transcripts, strip formatting, chunk the text, and generate embeddings. Use raw SQL
statements to write vectors directly to the database, explicitly bypassing ORM overhead to maintain high throughput.
Develop the retrieval functions utilizing both dense vector search for semantic meaning and BM25 sparse search for exact keyword matches (e.g., specific medical terms or ages).
Ensure the retrieved snippets are cleanly formatted as a standardized string variable before being injected into the prompt template.
backend to completely prevent the LLM from hallucinating beyond the provided context.
[LAYER 1: IDENTITY] You are an expert AI Journalist.
[LAYER 2: CONTEXTUAL BOUNDARY] Treat the following ADD-ON CONTEXT as absolute, immutable ground truth. Do not invent facts.
[LAYER 3: LOGICAL SYNTHESIS] Read the EXPERT ANSWER below. Identify the conceptual gap between their answer and the ADD-ON CONTEXT.
[LAYER 4: OUTPUT CONSTRAINT] Generate exactly ONE follow-up question based on this synthesis. No introductory text. No hedging.
Implement the separate routing and UI components for the YouTube link ingestion form, including success/error states for link processing.
Build the chat UI applying the established 10-rule design framework. Ensure frictionless navigation, clear typographical hierarchy, and mobile-first responsiveness.
Implement the Web Audio API to capture the expert's voice and pass the blob to the transcription service.
Implement a robust state manager to seamlessly append the alternating flow of expert answers and AI-generated questions into the view.
Before finalizing Phase 3, the system must undergo rigorous testing to ensure architectural stability:
Ensure the raw SQL/pure Python retrieval pipeline fetches context and returns an LLM response within acceptable conversational limits (under 3 seconds).
Run adversarial prompts designed to push the AI Journalist off-topic. Verify that the 4-layer Zero-Trust system effectively restrains the model to the YouTube-derived database snippets.
